name: Orange Cheesecake with Orange Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Cup toasted whole wheat bread crumbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a soufflé dish or 6-inch spring form pan with aluminum foil.
2. Decoratively arrange orange sections in the bottom of the dish.
3. Beat cream cheese until smooth.
4. Add 1/2 cup sugar and beat until combined.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Pour the mixture over the orange sections.
7. Sprinkle with bread crumbs.
8. Cover the dish securely with aluminum foil.
9. Place a cooking rack and 2 cups water in a 6-quart pressure cooker.
10. Place the dish on the rack.
11. Close the cover securely.
12. Place the pressure regulator on the vent pipe.
13. Cook for 20 minutes at 15 pounds pressure.
14. Allow the cooker to cool immediately.
15. Remove the cheesecake and let it cool in the soufflé dish on a wire rack.
16. Loosen the edges and carefully unmold.
17. Refrigerate until chilled.
18. Meanwhile, mix 1/4 cup sugar and cornstarch in a small saucepan over medium heat.
19. Stir in the reserved mandarin orange juice (from the can).
20. Cook, stirring, until the sauce boils and thickens.
21. Stir in the orange extract.
22. Allow to cool, then spoon the sauce over the cheesecake.
